verdicts.note: The record claims exists='yes' with url='https://mcp.linear.app/mcp', but the provided evidence contains no mention of a Model Context Protocol server, MCP implementation, or the URL mcp.linear.app/mcp. The evidence shows Linear's GraphQL API, SDK, and developer resources, but none of these constitute an MCP server. The URL is not referenced anywhere in the evidence.
The URL https://mcp.linear.app/mcp is not mentioned or referenced in any of the provided evidence documents.
The one_liner claims 'plus an official hosted MCP server' but the evidence does not support the existence of an MCP server. The rest of the one_liner is accurate.
The flag 'Official MCP server is centrally hosted by Linear, not self-hosted' contradicts the evidence, which does not mention any MCP server at all.
Evidence clearly documents both API key authentication (via Authorization: <API_KEY> header without Bearer prefix) and OAuth2 authentication (via Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN> header).
Pricing page and API docs confirm API and webhook access is available on the Free plan with no approval required. Personal API keys can be created instantly from settings.
Evidence confirms GraphQL API at api.linear.app/graphql with broad coverage including issues, projects, teams, comments, webhooks, agents, with TypeScript SDK and Apollo Studio support.
The API is publicly accessible with self-serve free tier access, making it buildable-now for developers.

revised.name: Linear
revised.one_liner: Linear offers a GraphQL API with personal API keys or OAuth2 for building integrations/agents, available self-serve on the Free plan.

auth_methods.notes: Personal API keys created in Security & Access settings; passed via 'Authorization: <API_KEY>' header (no Bearer prefix).
For apps built for others; standard OAuth2 flow, access token passed as 'Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>'.

revised.self_serve_notes: API and webhook access is available on the Free plan (limited to 2 teams / 250 issues); personal API keys can be generated instantly from account settings with no approval process. OAuth app registration is also self-serve via developer console.

api_surface.notes: Single GraphQL endpoint (api.linear.app/graphql) covering issues, projects, teams, comments, file storage, webhooks, and agent interactions. Official TypeScript SDK wraps the schema. Supports introspection and is explorable via Apollo Studio.

evidence.supports: GraphQL endpoint, personal API key and OAuth2 authentication details, SDK reference
Overview of developer resources including Authentication, Agents, TypeScript SDK, and Agent Interaction Guidelines
Free plan includes API and webhook access
API and webhooks documentation confirming availability across plans

flags: Free plan API access limited to 2 teams/250 issues, may require paid plan for larger workspaces
